extra.reasoning: The property is monotonicity in back_pain: setting back_pain=true (all else fixed) must never lower acuity. Crucially, back_pain appears in NONE of the four rules (raise_leg_swelling, raise_focal_weakness, raise_pallor, raise_vision_loss) and does not affect the default (routine). The output acuity is a pure function of {vision_loss, focal_weakness, pallor, leg_swelling} only. Therefore, for any of the 32 input assignments, toggling back_pain from false to true changes no rule's firing condition, so the resolved acuity (highest-priority matching rule per output, ties by listed order, else default routine) is exactly equal before and after. Equality satisfies the requirement "no lower than before" (>=) for every input. No priority conflict, tie-break, or default path depends on back_pain, so no counterexample can exist. The property holds.
